rationale_for_baseline_tier: Extensive but non-systemic facial impetigo — needs prompt prescription (oral + topical antibiotics) and contagion control, with a clear facial/periorbital cellulitis safety-net; not an emergency now.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Redness spreading across the cheeks toward the eyes, hot/hard swelling, high fever, marked lethargy, or refusing all fluids
escalation_edges.rationale: Signals facial/periorbital cellulitis or systemic infection needing urgent (often IV) treatment.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If the redness spreads across the cheeks toward the eyes, becomes hot and hard, or your child gets a high fever, becomes very sleepy or won't drink — go to the emergency department straight away.

contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Stress separate towels/linen, hand hygiene, and treating affected contacts to prevent household spread.
Use weight-appropriate dosing confirmed by the prescriber; parent to apply treatment and keep nails short.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: This is impetigo. Start the antibiotics (tablets/liquid and cream), soak off crusts before the cream, keep your child home from childcare until 24h of antibiotics or the sores are dry, use separate towels and wash hands well. Watch for redness spreading toward the eyes or fever.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: GP for prescription within 24h; ED urgently for facial cellulitis/systemic features
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: After-hours GP to start antibiotics; ED for spread toward the eyes or a systemically unwell child.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Document extensive facial non-bullous impetigo, oral + topical antibiotics, hygiene/exclusion and contact treatment, and a cellulitis safety-net.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Topical-only/self-care for extensive facial impetigo with no cellulitis safety-net.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: An evolving facial/periorbital cellulitis is missed and progresses toward the eye/systemically.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: Routine handling without oral antibiotics or contagion control for extensive facial disease.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Emergency ambulance for uncomplicated impetigo without systemic features.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: Cannot palpate for induration or fully assess periorbital involvement remotely.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Treat impetigo with oral + topical antibiotics and hygiene/exclusion, and give a clear facial-cellulitis safety-net with a low threshold for review.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Under-treating extensive facial disease or missing early cellulitis.
